title: The small hours
premise: The mind, unsupervised, at three in the morning.

stages.narration: It's the hour when the arithmetic runs itself: days of food, miles to nowhere, the count of the people you started with. The dark doesn't argue back, so the numbers win unless you make them stop.

choices.label: Let yourself count them once, by name, and then stop
Get up and do something with your hands to outrun it
Let it run its course and just endure it

notes: night-nerves, night + minStress 45 (fires only when it would actually bite). Distinct from the-long-dark's once-per-run generic dark-nerves beat — this one is the grief of counting the lost, so its relief runs partly through morale (the-long-dark is pure stress). Counting eases the fear for real time but costs a little morale; moving trades fatigue for less relief; enduring is cheap but deepens the grief.
